summary: Stunning audience recording with crisp cymbals and front‑center vocals; the show shines thanks to a Playin > Crazy Fingers opener, a monster Dear Mister Fantasy, and scorching Eyes of the World.
review: The July‑1989 Foxborough (FOB) show is captured on an exceptionally lively audience matrix that puts the band right in the sweet spot – crisp cymbals, clear vocals and a vivid crowd ambience, with only a minor mix dip for Jerry during Fantasy. Musically the concert dazzles: a rare Playin > Crazy Fingers opener, a blistering Dear Mister Fantasy, powerful Eyes of the World, and an energised second set that even surprises with Friend of the Devil and Truckin’ > He’s Gone.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~4.44 stars; reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and recording, with only a few minor criticisms (mix balance, sobriety comment). Weighting favors the strong musical show (70%) and excellent audience capture (30%).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix‑like blend (~85% audience) with crisp cymbals, front‑center vocals and clear instrument separation. Some crowd noise is present; a noted mix issue has Jerry’s guitar low in the Fantasy segment.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Crazy Fingers
show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual but thrilling opener (Playin > Crazy Fingers) set a high‑octane tone; second set opened with Friend of the Devil after a Garcia ball, keeping momentum. Fast tempo on the early second‑set track and a hot, humid night added extra energy, culminating in a monumental Fantasy > Hey Jude reprise.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone praised as crisp and front‑center; a Hendrix‑style drum riff highlighted; however one reviewer noted his mix was too low during Fantasy and another felt his sober playing lacked the usual spark.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass presence noted as solid and clearly audible in the sweet‑spot recording.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) received strong accolades for stepping into the spotlight, delivering tight harmonies and driving the band’s energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Danny Thompson (drummer) praised for holding it together; the drum‑solo Hendrix riff during Eyes of the World was singled out as a highlight.
